question.text: What is "inflation risk" for a retired person?

answer.text: The risk that rising prices will erode the value of their retirement savings over time
The risk that the government will increase taxes on retirement income
The risk that the stock market will crash
The risk that the bank will reduce FD interest rates

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Inflation risk is the danger that rising prices of everyday goods (groceries, medicines, utilities) will gradually reduce what your retirement savings can buy. If your investments earn 5% but inflation is 7%, you are effectively losing 2% of your purchasing power every year. **The Trap:** Tax increases and FD rate reductions are real concerns, but they are different types of risk — not specifically "inflation risk." A stock market crash is "market risk." **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under risk types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Inflation risk is the biggest hidden enemy of retirement savings. Help your clients understand that keeping all their money in low-interest savings accounts may feel safe but actually loses value over time due to inflation.
